description: The soft curly hair forming the coat of sheep; the warmest natural fiber. 'Mophi' names what sheep give—the gift of warmth, renewable each year.
sound_symbolism: The word warms soft. 'Mo' masses with insulating bulk, warmth held. Then 'phi' /ɸi/ breathes with soft airiness, fiber gentle.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun; a mass noun, so it rarely takes 'lo' (PL). The warmest natural fiber, sheared from sheep. Becomes 'sima' (thread) and then 'wetha' (cloth).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Gentleness as warmth—wool as soft protection, care made material.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Wool warmed humanity—the renewable fiber sheared each spring, warmth without killing.
